name: El Paso
summary: El Paso ranks as one of the safest large cities in the United States with violent crime rates significantly below national averages. While property crimes like auto theft are trending upward, the city maintains its reputation for overall safety with strong community policing efforts.
quickVerdict: El Paso is genuinely one of America's safest large cities. With violent crime rates far below national averages and a 36% lower overall crime rate than typical US cities, tourists can feel confident exploring this vibrant border city. The main concerns are property crimes including car theft, which has seen an uptick recently. Downtown, university areas, and major tourist attractions are generally safe, though visitors should exercise normal urban precautions, especially at night.

neighborhoods.name: Lancaster
neighborhoods.description: Consistently rated as one of El Paso's safest neighborhoods with very low crime rates and well-maintained residential areas.

neighborhoods.name: Upper Vista Real
neighborhoods.description: Upscale westside area with excellent safety ratings and popular with families and professionals.

neighborhoods.name: Kohlberg
neighborhoods.description: Quiet residential neighborhood known for low crime rates and strong community involvement.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown El Paso
neighborhoods.description: Main tourist and business district with good daytime safety but requires more caution at night, especially around entertainment areas.

neighborhoods.name: Cincinnati Entertainment District
neighborhoods.description: Popular nightlife area near UTEP with higher crime rates due to bars and restaurants, though generally manageable with precautions.

neighborhoods.name: Angel's Triangle
neighborhoods.description: Historically known as one of El Paso's higher-crime areas, though significantly improved from past decades. Best avoided by tourists.

scams.name: Fake Police Officers
scams.description: Scammers impersonate police officers demanding money or asking for donations, particularly targeting tourists unfamiliar with proper procedures.
scams.howToAvoid: Real police will have proper identification and never demand immediate cash payments. Ask to see credentials and request to speak with a supervisor if approached.

scams.name: Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Taxi drivers claim broken meters or take unnecessarily long routes, especially from the airport. Some drivers also recommend specific hotels for commission.
scams.howToAvoid: Use ride-sharing apps like Uber or Lyft, agree on fares upfront if no meter, and research typical taxi costs before travel.

scams.name: Border Crossing Assistance Scams
scams.description: People pose as official border agents or visa officers offering assistance for crossing into Mexico for a fee.
scams.howToAvoid: Only deal with official uniformed border personnel at designated crossing points. Never pay unofficial individuals for border assistance.

scams.name: Too-Good-To-Be-True Deals
scams.description: Various street vendors or online offers promising unrealistic discounts on tours, accommodations, or services in the border area.
scams.howToAvoid: Book through reputable agencies, verify deals independently, and be suspicious of pressure tactics or requests for immediate payment.

soloFemale.overview: El Paso is considered one of the safest cities in the US for solo female travelers. Many women report feeling comfortable walking alone during the day and most areas are well-lit and monitored. The city's low violent crime rates and strong community policing make it particularly welcoming for independent female travelers.
tips: Stay in well-lit, populated areas especially after dark
Use rideshare apps rather than flagging down taxis at night
Trust your instincts and avoid isolated areas in entertainment districts
Consider staying near downtown or westside neighborhoods with better safety ratings

nightSafety.overview: Downtown El Paso and main tourist areas are generally safe at night with good lighting and regular police presence. The Cincinnati Entertainment District near UTEP can get crowded and rowdy on weekends, requiring extra caution. Most residents feel comfortable in central areas after dark, though some neighborhoods should be avoided.
tips: Stick to main streets and well-lit areas
Avoid the Cincinnati District late at night if you're not familiar with the area
Use rideshare services instead of walking long distances
Stay aware of your surroundings especially around bars and clubs

transport.metro: Sun Metro operates city buses with real-time tracking and mobile ticketing. The system includes regular buses and Brio express routes. El Paso County is launching a zero-fare pilot program through September 2026. The historic streetcar connects downtown to UTEP campus.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Lyft operate throughout El Paso with good coverage. Both services are considered safe and reliable. The city has designated rideshare zones in popular nightlife areas to improve pickup safety.
transport.taxis: Traditional taxis are available but less regulated than rideshare options. Some drivers may attempt overcharging, especially from the airport. Always agree on fare upfront or ensure the meter is working.
transport.tips: Use rideshare apps for best safety and transparency. Public transit is generally safe during daytime but limited at night. The Sun Metro app provides real-time bus tracking and mobile payments.

customs: El Paso has a strong Mexican-American cultural influence with many bilingual residents
Tipping 18-20% at restaurants and 15-18% for services is standard
The city observes both American holidays and some Mexican celebrations like Dia de los Muertos
Many businesses close earlier on Sundays, and some close for traditional siesta hours in smaller establishments

health.overview: El Paso has good healthcare infrastructure with several major hospitals and clinics. The city operates public health services including vaccination programs for uninsured residents. Desert climate requires attention to hydration and sun protection.
health.water: Tap water is safe to drink and meets all federal standards. The El Paso Water utility maintains high quality standards and regular testing.
health.vaccinations: Standard US vaccinations are recommended. No special vaccines required for El Paso, though travelers planning to cross into Mexico should consult CDC guidelines for Mexico-specific recommendations.
health.altitude: El Paso sits at 3,800 feet elevation, which may cause mild adjustment issues for some visitors. The desert climate features hot, dry summers and mild winters. Stay hydrated and use sun protection year-round.

emergency.usEmbassy: No US embassy in El Paso. For US citizen services, contact the nearest US consulate in Ciudad Juarez, Mexico at +52-656-227-3000, or the State Department at 1-888-407-4747.

faq.q: Is El Paso safe for tourists?
faq.a: Yes, El Paso is one of the safest large cities in the United States with crime rates 36% below national averages. Tourist areas are well-patrolled and most visitors have no safety issues.

faq.q: Is El Paso safe at night?
faq.a: Downtown and main tourist areas are generally safe at night with good lighting and police presence. The entertainment districts require more caution, especially on weekends.

faq.q: Is El Paso safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Absolutely. El Paso is considered one of the best US cities for solo female travel with low violent crime rates and a welcoming community atmosphere.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in El Paso?
faq.a: Angel's Triangle neighborhood has higher crime rates and should be avoided. Some eastern areas and late-night entertainment districts require extra caution.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in El Paso?
faq.a: Yes, Sun Metro buses and the streetcar system are generally safe and reliable. The upcoming zero-fare program will make public transit even more accessible.
